_comment: Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) Web log field inventory, scored against OCSF HTTP Activity (4002). ZIA web logs are delivered via the NSS feed (or Cloud NSS to S3); the feed is customizable, so this inventory scopes to the documented standard Web-log field set (the %s{...}/%d{...} tokens in Zscaler's 'NSS Feed Output Format: Web Logs'). Field names follow the documented schema; the NSS token for each is in the note. Leaf/atomic fields only. Transcribed from Zscaler's NSS Web Log feed-format reference (the downloadable feed-format CSV); corroborated against Panther (Zscaler.ZIA.WebLog), Elastic (zscaler_zia), and Rapid7. An official Zscaler ZIA->OCSF mapping for AWS Security Lake exists at the CLASS level (Web Logs -> HTTP Activity 4002, OCSF v1.5.0) but its field-level carry list is not publicly retrievable, so no per-field `official` flag is carried (see PROVENANCE.md). See PROVENANCE.md.
source_schema: Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) Web log (NSS feed output format)

fields.note: time/date of the transaction
%s{login}; user's login name in email format
%s{dept}; department of the user
%s{location}; Zscaler gateway location / sub-location of the source (a topology/policy object, not geo)
%s{url}; destination URL (excluding protocol identifier)
%s{host}; destination hostname from the request line/header
%s{urlcat}; category of the destination URL (Zscaler taxonomy)
%s{urlclass}; class of the destination URL (Zscaler taxonomy, e.g. Bandwidth Loss)
%s{urlsupercat}; super-category of the destination URL (Zscaler taxonomy)
%s{appname}; name of the cloud application accessed
%s{appclass}; class/category of the cloud application (CASB taxonomy)
%s{action}; Allowed or Blocked
%s{reason}; action taken and the policy applied if blocked
%s{reqmethod}; HTTP request method (get, connect, ...)
%d{reqsize}; total HTTP request size in bytes
%d{respsize}; total HTTP response size in bytes
%d{totalsize}; sum of request and response sizes
%s{respcode}; HTTP response code sent to the client
%s{ua}; full user-agent string
%s{referer}; HTTP referrer URL
%s{cip}; IP address of the user (internal or visible)
%s{cpubip}; client public (egress/NAT) IP address
%s{sip}; destination server IP address
%s{contenttype}; response content type (MIME)
%s{threatname}; name of the detected threat
%s{malwarecat}; malware category detected (Zscaler taxonomy)
%s{malwareclass}; class of malware detected (Zscaler taxonomy)
%d{riskscore}; Page Risk Index of the destination URL (0-100)
%s{dlpdict}; DLP dictionaries matched
%s{dlpeng}; DLP engine matched
%s{filetype}; type of the downloaded file (Zscaler taxonomy)
%s{fileclass}; class of the downloaded file (Zscaler taxonomy)
%s{rulelabel}; name of the policy rule applied
%s{devicehostname}; hostname of the user's device
%s{deviceowner}; owner of the user's device
%s{proto}; protocol type of the transaction (HTTP/HTTPS/...)
